plan_history.intent_description: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 has a duplex/speed autonegotiation mismatch with its peer (R1) causing input_errors and potential inbound packet loss; desired outcome is to stop input_errors by aligning link negotiation so the interface operates without errors.
plan_history.problem: Duplex/speed autonegotiation mismatch on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 producing input_errors (observed 13 since last check, total 44) and risking inbound packet loss.
plan_history.fix_summary: Enable autonegotiation on router2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 (set speed and duplex to auto / remove hard-set 100 Mbps half-duplex) to match R1. Operational risk: brief traffic disruption on the interface while settings apply.

plan_history.intent_description: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is forced to 100Mbps half-duplex while its peer R1 is autonegotiating, causing input_errors and inbound packet loss. Desired outcome: align negotiation so the interface negotiates/operates without input errors.
plan_history.problem: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 has forced speed/duplex (100 Mbps, half) with autonegotiation disabled while the peer (R1) is set to autonegotiation, producing input_errors and packet loss.
plan_history.fix_summary: Enable autonegotiation on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 and set interface speed and duplex to auto to match the peer and eliminate input errors.

remaining_issues: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 still has forced speed=100 and duplex=half with autonegotiation disabled while the peer is configured for autonegotiation — this mismatch remains and is producing input_errors/inbound packet loss.

Configuration drift: interface description indicates intent to enable autonegotiation, but the active config on R2 still forces speed/duplex, so the intended change has not been applied or did not persist.

args.description: Autonegotiation enabled; speed auto; duplex auto; removed explicit speed/duplex
